examples: An example is narcolepsy, in which emotionally laden events trigger sudden cataplexy.
Cataplexy can be reduced by antidepressant drugs, which suppress rapid eye movement sleep.
Narcolepsy affects around 30, 000 people in the UK and about 70 per cent of them also have cataplexy.
Worries about the potential for abuse have led the FDA to approve the drug only for narcoleptics who suffer from cataplexy.
Cataplexy can range in severity, from drooping face muscles to full-body immobility.
